Arrived in Brussels. Ahead today is a meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, and tomorrow with Prime Minister of Belgium Bart De Wever. An audience with King Philippe of the Belgians is also scheduled.

These are important days for strengthening our defense: there will be meetings with the defense ministers of the United Kingdom and Germany, a Ramstein meeting, and a meeting of the European Council.

I thank everyone who is helping and supporting us. Europe must be strong and united. This will largely determine how this war ends.

On my way to Brussels, I spoke with @alexstubb. We discussed the outcomes of the G7 Summit in France and the key agreements reached. If everything we discussed at this Summit and in the G7 meetings is implemented, it will clearly bring positive results for our countries, for Europe, and globally. 

Of course, the key priority for us is to end Russia’s war against Ukraine with a dignified peace and to stabilize international relations. This can only be achieved together — the United States, Ukraine, and the whole of Europe, our key partners in the G7 and worldwide. Europe must be strongly represented in the negotiations. In the coming days, we will try to begin implementing some new ideas. 

I thank everyone who is helping! Thank you, Alex!
